laws.title: Public Law 24-176 | Comprehensive Federal Government Funding Act
laws.required_code_treatment: retain only source-limited history; exclude appropriations from permanent Code text

source_evidence.status_basis: Source was deleted or not found, so no operative text could be audited.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a source-limited historical note and flag the source for reacquisition.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is unavailable, so chronology supports only a historical-note disposition and no verified operative-code rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 24-177 | SS Jurisdiction Act
laws.required_code_treatment: retain only source-limited history; no permanent Code text

source_evidence.status_basis: Source was deleted or not found, so no lawful codification conclusion can be drawn.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a source-limited historical note and flag the source for reacquisition.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is unavailable, so chronology supports only a historical-note disposition and no verified operative-code rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 24-178 | Equality in Employment Act
laws.required_code_treatment: keep the 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) amendment and a concise statutory note; do not carry a full-text dump

current_implementation.branch_vs_origin: HEAD includes the amendment text and note; origin/main does not show the same insertion

source_evidence.status_basis: SEC 6(a)(1)-(2) expressly amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) by adding gender identity and sexual orientation.

laws.recommended_action: Leave the Title 42 amendment in place and keep only a concise statutory note for the amendment history.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-179 | Fiscal Year 21-2
laws.required_code_treatment: treat SEC 5 as a temporary note at 2 U.S.C. 4501 and exclude appropriations from permanent Code text

current_implementation.branch_vs_origin: HEAD contains the statutory note; origin/main does not show the same note

source_evidence.status_basis: The act is a short-term appropriations measure and SEC 5 is a temporary COLA suspension under 2 U.S.C. 4501.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a concise historical note and preserve SEC 5 as a temporary note at 2 U.S.C. 4501.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-180 | Sanction Repealment Act
laws.required_code_treatment: record the PL 9-55 repeal at the target's home and keep SEC 3(c) as a concise note

source_evidence.status_basis: SEC 3(b) repeals PL 9-55 in its entirety and SEC 3(c) declares the sanctions null and void.

laws.recommended_action: Add the repeal-marking at the PL 9-55 home and keep the sanctions-nullification as a concise statutory note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-181 | Federal District of Columbia Criminal Code
laws.required_code_treatment: keep only a current local statutory note at /us/usc/t40/s101/4; do not convert the local code into permanent U.S. Code text

current_implementation.branch_vs_origin: HEAD is still not aligned with the source treatment; origin/main also lacks a corrected local note

source_evidence.status_basis: SEC 4 makes the attached criminal code the Criminal Code for the District of Columbia and the operative provisions are local code material.

laws.recommended_action: Replace the repealed/superseded label with a current local statutory note at /us/usc/t40/s101/4.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-182 | Pentagon Expansion and National Territory Authorization Act
laws.required_code_treatment: retain only source-limited history until the actual statutory text is recovered

source_evidence.status_basis: The attachment resolves to viewer debris and no operative statutory text could be recovered or verified.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a source-limited historical note and do not invent any operative language.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is unavailable, so chronology supports only a historical-note disposition and no verified operative-code rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 25-183 | Commerce and Labor Advancement Act (CLAA)
laws.required_code_treatment: keep only repeal/history because PL 31-226 repeals the law in full

source_evidence.later_law: PL-031-226 repeals PL-025-183 in its entirety
source_evidence.status_basis: The source establishes a licensing framework, but the later repeal controls final codification.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the law only as superseded history and do not insert operative Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-184 | Legal Advisors, Representatives, and Professionals Exam Regulations (LARPER)
laws.required_code_treatment: keep only repeal/history because PL 33-238 repeals the law in full

source_evidence.later_law: PL-033-238 repeals PL-025-184 in its entirety
source_evidence.status_basis: The act creates a licensing framework in the source, but later repeal makes it historical only.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the law only as superseded history and do not insert operative Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.

laws.title: Public Law 25-185 | Fiscal Year 21-3
laws.required_code_treatment: exclude from permanent Code text and retain only a concise historical note if desired

current_implementation.branch_vs_origin: HEAD contains the note, but the act remains expired appropriations history

source_evidence.status_basis: The act is a one-year appropriations measure for fiscal year 21-3 and contains no permanent positive-law amendment.

laws.recommended_action: Reclassify the measure as expired appropriations history and remove any implication that it is current Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source evidence supports the stated disposition, with the current implementation reflecting the surviving code history.
